---
name: auto-coder
description: Autonomous spec-driven development agent for ChipWise Enterprise. Syncs ENTERPRISE_DEV_SPEC.md + DEVELOPMENT_PLAN.md into chapter-based reference files, identifies the next pending task from the plan, implements code following spec architecture and patterns, runs tests with up to 3 auto-fix rounds, and marks the task complete with an atomic commit. Use when user says "auto code", "自动开发", "自动写代码", "auto dev", "一键开发", "autopilot", "开始开发", or wants fully automated spec-to-code workflow.
---

# Auto Coder — ChipWise Enterprise

One trigger completes **sync specs → find task → implement → test → persist progress**.

Optional modifiers: append a task ID (e.g. `auto code 1A3`) to target a specific task, or `--no-commit` to skip git commit.

> **Platform**: Primary development is on **Windows** (AMD Ryzen AI 395). Use PowerShell syntax by default; fall back to bash only in Docker/CI contexts.

---

## Pipeline

```
Sync Spec → Find Task → Implement → Test (≤3 fix rounds) → Persist
```

Pause only at the end for commit confirmation. Run everything else autonomously.

> **⚠️ Environment checks (run before first command):**
> 1. **venv** — if `.venv` exists, activate it:
>    - **Windows**: `.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1`
>    - **macOS/Linux**: `source .venv/bin/activate`
>    - If `.venv` does not exist yet (pre-1A1), skip — task 1A1 creates it.
> 2. **git** — if `.git` exists, standard workflow. If not, the Persist step will offer to run `git init`.
> 3. **CLAUDE.md** — read the project root `CLAUDE.md` for additional context (architecture overview, conventions, troubleshooting). It is the single source of truth for AI assistant context.

## Reference Map

All files under `.github/skills/auto-coder/references/` (auto-generated by sync_spec.py):

| File | Source | Content | When to Read |
|------|--------|---------|-------------|
| `00-metadata.md` | DEV_SPEC preamble | Version, changelog, table of contents | Version checks, changelog lookup |
| `01-overview.md` | DEV_SPEC §1 | Project overview & goals | First task or when needing project context |
| `02-features.md` | DEV_SPEC §2 | Core features & design decisions | Feature-related tasks |
| `03-tech-stack.md` | DEV_SPEC §3 | Tech stack & library selections | Library/pattern choices |
| `04-architecture.md` | DEV_SPEC §4 | System architecture, schemas, APIs, Agent, Pipelines (~200KB) | Creating/modifying any module — **read targeted subsections, not the whole file** |
| `05-testing.md` | DEV_SPEC §5 | Testing conventions, CI/CD, observability | Writing tests or adding metrics |
| `06-schedule.md` | DEV_SPEC §6 header + DEVELOPMENT_PLAN.md | Phase overview + full task plan with per-task specs (~151KB) | Every cycle — **read progress tables first, grep for specific task** |
| `07-appendix.md` | DEV_SPEC §7 | settings.yaml schema, API directory, glossary | Config-related tasks or looking up terms |

## Navigating Large References

`04-architecture.md` (~200KB) and `06-schedule.md` (~151KB) are too large to read in full. Use targeted reading:

### 06-schedule.md (Finding Tasks)

```
Lines 1-260    → Progress tracking tables (all 78 tasks, status at a glance)
Line  ~255+    → Detailed task specs (one per task)
```

To find a specific task's details, grep for its heading:
```powershell
grep -n "### 1A3" .github/skills/auto-coder/references/06-schedule.md
```

Each task detail block follows this structure:
```
### {TASK_ID}：{Task Name}
- **目标**：(goal description)
- **修改/创建文件**：(list of files to create/modify)
- **实现类/函数**：(function signatures with implementation details)
- **验收标准**：(acceptance criteria as bullet points)
- **测试方法**：(test command, e.g. `pytest -q tests/unit/test_xxx.py`)
```

### 04-architecture.md (Phase-Targeted Reading)

Read only the subsections relevant to your current phase:

| Phase | Relevant §4 subsections | Topics |
|-------|------------------------|--------|
| **Phase 1** | §4.1-4.4 | Architecture overview, request flows, directory tree (delivery checklist) |
| **Phase 2** | §4.7, §4.8 | Database schemas (PG/Milvus/Kùzu), Agent architecture (ReAct, Tools) |
| **Phase 3** | §4.6 | Data engineering pipeline (3-tier extraction, Celery chain, ingestion flows) |
| **Phase 4-5** | §4.8, §4.9 | Agent tools, pipeline definitions |
| **Phase 6** | §4.5, §4.10-4.12, §5 | Deployment, conversation/cache, concurrency, SSO |

Use `grep -n "## 4.7"` to locate subsection start lines, then `view_range` to read just that section.

---

### 1. Sync Spec

```powershell
python .github/skills/auto-coder/scripts/sync_spec.py
```

This reads **two sources**:
- `docs/ENTERPRISE_DEV_SPEC.md` → generates `00-metadata`, `01-05` and `07` reference files
- `docs/DEVELOPMENT_PLAN.md` → generates `06-schedule.md` (§6 overview header + full task plan)

Then read the schedule file to get task statuses:
- Read `.github/skills/auto-coder/references/06-schedule.md`

Task markers (in markdown table cells, e.g. `| [x] |`):

| Marker | Status |
|--------|--------|
| `[ ]` / `⬜` | Not started |
| `[~]` / `🔶` / `(进行中)` | In progress |
| `[x]` / `✅` / `(已完成)` | Completed |

---

### 2. Find Task

Task IDs follow the format **`<Phase><Subphase><Number>`** — e.g. `1A1`, `1B3`, `2C4`.
- Phase 1-6 correspond to the six development phases
- Subphase letter (A, B, C…) corresponds to the sub-stage within the phase
- Number is the sequential task within the sub-stage

**Efficient task search** (don't read the entire 151KB file):
1. Read the first ~260 lines of `06-schedule.md` (progress tracking tables)
2. Scan for first `[~]` (in-progress), else first `[ ]` (not started) — pick that task ID
3. If user specified a task ID (e.g. `auto code 1A3`), use that directly

**Load the task detail:**
```powershell
grep -n "### {TASK_ID}" .github/skills/auto-coder/references/06-schedule.md
```
Then `view_range` from that line to the next `---` separator to read the full task spec.

Quick-check predecessor artifacts exist (file-level only). On mismatch, log a warning and continue — only stop if the target task itself is blocked.

---

### 3. Implement

1. **Read relevant spec** from `.github/skills/auto-coder/references/`:
   - Architecture & module design: `04-architecture.md` — **read only the subsections relevant to the current phase** (see "Navigating Large References" above); §4.4 directory tree is the delivery checklist
   - Tech stack details: `03-tech-stack.md`
   - Testing conventions: `05-testing.md`
   - Settings schema: `07-appendix.md` (when implementing config-related code)
   - Project conventions: root `CLAUDE.md` (for coding patterns, troubleshooting, key conventions)

2. **Extract** from `06-schedule.md` for the target task: inputs/outputs, file list, function signatures, acceptance criteria.

3. **Plan** files to create/modify before writing any code.

4. **Code** — project-specific rules:
   - Treat `docs/ENTERPRISE_DEV_SPEC.md` as single source of truth for architecture
   - Use `config/settings.yaml` values via `src/core/settings.py`, never hardcode
   - Follow Factory + Pluggable Abstractions pattern: `Base*` → Implementation → `*Factory`
   - Match existing codebase patterns and style
   - Every request path must carry `TraceContext` and call `trace.record_stage()`
   - Graceful degradation: optional components (Reranker, Graph, Cache) must have `disabled`/`None` mode

5. **Write tests** alongside code:
   - Unit tests → `tests/unit/` (mock all external deps: LLM, Milvus, Redis, PostgreSQL)
   - Integration tests → `tests/integration/` (requires Docker services)
   - Mark with `@pytest.mark.unit` or `@pytest.mark.integration`

6. **Self-review** before running tests: verify all planned files exist and tests import correctly.

---

### 4. Test & Auto-Fix

```
Round 0..2:
  Run pytest on relevant test file(s)
  If pass → go to step 5
  If fail → analyze error, apply fix, re-run

Round 3 still failing → STOP, show failure report to user
```

**Test strategy by type:**
- **Unit tests** (`tests/unit/`): Always run. No Docker required. Mock all external deps.
- **Integration tests** (`tests/integration/`): Only run if Docker services are healthy (`docker-compose ps`). If Docker is not running, warn and skip — don't fail the task.
- **Syntax check**: `python -m compileall src` as a quick sanity pass before pytest.

---

### 5. Persist

1. **Update `docs/DEVELOPMENT_PLAN.md`**:
   - Change the task marker from `| [ ] |` → `| [x] |` in the progress tracking table row
   - Fill in the completion date column: `| [x] | 2026-04-10 |` (use today's date)
   - Update the **📈 总体进度** summary table (lines ~241-251): increment `已完成` count and recalculate `进度` percentage for the corresponding Phase
2. **Re-sync**: `python .github/skills/auto-coder/scripts/sync_spec.py --force`
   (This refreshes `06-schedule.md` to reflect the updated task status)
3. **Git operations** (conditional):
   - If `.git` does **not** exist: ask user whether to run `git init -b main && git add -A && git commit -m "..."`.
   - If `.git` exists: proceed with `git add` + `git commit` normally.
4. **Show summary & ask**:

```
✅ [1A3] 配置加载与校验 — done
   Files: src/core/settings.py, tests/unit/test_settings.py
   Tests: 8/8 passed
   Commit: feat(config): [1A3] implement settings loader and validation

   "commit" → git add + commit
   "skip"   → end (no commit)
   "next"   → commit + start next task
```

On "next", loop back to step 1 and start the next task.

---

## Quick Reference: 78 Tasks Across 6 Phases

| Phase | Tasks | Focus |
|-------|-------|-------|
| **1** (Week 1-2) | 1A1-1E3 (18) | Project skeleton, Docker infra, model services, FastAPI gateway, Kùzu + Agent skeleton |
| **2** (Week 3-4) | 2A1-2D3 (19) | Libs pluggable layer, Hybrid+Graph retrieval, Agent ReAct loop, 3 foundational tools |
| **3** (Week 5-7) | 3A1-3C4 (13) | PDF extraction, Celery task chain, 3 ingestion sources (upload/watchdog/crawler) |
| **4** (Week 8-10) | 4A1-4C3 (8) | chip_compare, chip_select, bom_review tools |
| **5** (Week 11-13) | 5A1-5D2 (9) | test_case_gen, design_rule_check, knowledge_search, report_export tools |
| **6** (Week 14-16) | 6A1-6D3 (11) | Gradio UI, SSO/OIDC, load testing, E2E testing, documentation |
